overview: Generates an analysis report on the elements present in a set of input XML documents. The report helps guess localization-related aspects of the input documents, such as which elements should have their content extracted, which elements are likely to be inline, and whether there are any CDATA sections. Expects raw documents and passes them through unchanged.

outputPath.help: Full path (including filename) where the HTML analysis report will be written. The report summarizes element usage across all processed XML documents to help determine localization properties.
outputPath.values: A file system path ending in `.html`. Can be absolute or relative to the working directory.

autoOpen.help: Automatically opens the generated report file in the system's default browser/application after the pipeline finishes processing all documents.

processingNotes: The step passes raw documents through unchanged — it only reads and analyzes the XML content without modifying it.
The report aggregates information across **all** XML documents processed in the batch, not just a single file.
The generated report is HTML format and covers element inventory, inline element candidates, and CDATA section detection.

examples.title: Basic XML analysis
examples.description: Run the step with default settings to generate an `analysis.html` report that opens automatically after completion. Use the report to determine which elements contain translatable content and which are inline before configuring an XML filter.
